events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario describes a reverse-lapped WRB (upper layer behind lower), which is a clear sequence violation. WRB must be installed shingle-fashion so upper layers lap over lower layers to shed water. Reverse lapping means water will be directed behind the lower layer, causing water intrusion.

Decision: fail
Risk: high (water intrusion risk on commercial building)
Work stage: in-progress
Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant
Percent complete: estimate maybe 40%
Value: 0
Confidence: high, say 0.95
Order: not applicable for this task
Workable: empty
Findings: reverse-lapped WRB, upper layer behind lower, violates shingle-fashion requirement
Actions: remove and reinstall WRB with proper shingle-fashion laps per mfr instructions
Rationale: reverse lap directs water behind WRB, causing water intrusion
References: IRC R703, mfr instructions

    "Stop WRB and flashing work in the affected area immediately.",
    "Remove the improperly lapped WRB layers back to a point where correct shingle-fashion laps can be re-established.",
    "Reinstall WRB with upper courses lapping over lower courses per manufacturer instructions and IRC R703.",
    "Verify integration with flashing, penetrations, and openings before proceeding.",
    "Request third-party inspection of corrected WRB installation before covering or continuing."
  ],
  "rationale": "WRB must be installed shingle-fashion so water sheds downward and outward. A reverse lap directs water behind the barrier, which is a code and manufacturer non-compliance that will lead to water intrusion. Work cannot continue until the defective lap is removed and corrected.",

}
